title: Adiantum microphyllum
taxonomy: Adiantum microphyllum belongs to the family Pteridaceae and is a species of fern. It is a type of maidenhair fern, which is part of the genus Adiantum. The classification of A. microphyllum is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Pteridales, Family: Pteridaceae, Genus: Adiantum, Species: A. microphyllum. This classification places it within the larger group of vascular plants. As a member of the Pteridaceae family, it shares characteristics with other ferns.

intro: Adiantum microphyllum, also known as the small-leaf maidenhair fern, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Adiantaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help to reduce pain, improve respiratory health, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Adiantum microphyllum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Adiantum microphyllum is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including bronchitis, coughs, and skin irritations.
